Name: Keepers of the Old Faith
Signature Gear: ranger / peasant armour set + relic-hammer + reliquary shield
AI Doctrine: Expand by claiming sacred ruins and old churches. Build chapel-camps with relic vaults. Train NPCs in Combat/Hammers + Crafting/Armour + Township/Diplomacy. Will raid Scavenger junkyards monthly to ‘reclaim relics.’ Avoid Forgotten territory (they consider the marshes haunted).
Pledge Reward: Relic-hammer schematic + free passage through Highland gates + Keeper Diplomat hire dialogue unlocked.
Notes: Religious traditionalists. Pledging requires you renounce all Scavenger and Hollow Lord ties on the spot.

Name: Tech-Scavengers
Signature Gear: ranger / scavenger armour + ranged-tier weapons + attachment recipes
AI Doctrine: Expand by claiming pre-fall industrial sites. Build workshop-camps with extra workbenches + Tinkering benches. Train NPCs in Combat/Pistol+Rifle + Crafting/Gunsmith+Tinkering + Gathering/Mining. Skirmish weekly with Keepers over relic sites; trade openly with Hollow and Network.
Pledge Reward: Full Tinkering recipe set (all 7 attachments) + Gunsmith tier-2 unlock + scrap-cutter signature item.
Notes: Pragmatists. Pledging unlocks the deepest tech tree but locks you out of Keeper relic dialogues forever.

Name: Hollow Lords
Signature Gear: hollow plate + warhammer / heavy melee + shield-tower
AI Doctrine: Expand aggressively by raiding other camps and converting survivors into Hollow conscripts. Build slave-camp grids with palisade rings. Train NPCs in Combat/Hammers+Blades + Township/Defenses + Survival/Endurance. Will raid any flagged camp in Pit territory radius. Buys captives from Scavengers for parts.
Pledge Reward: Warhammer master schematic + +25% raid party size cap + Hollow Captain hire dialogue unlocked.
Notes: Warlords + slavers. The ‘bad guy’ pledge — drops you to Hated with Keepers and Network instantly, but Hollow gives you brutal combat power.

Name: Network
Signature Gear: spy gear / cloak + concealed pistols + lockpick set
AI Doctrine: Expand quietly by claiming radio relay sites and high points. Build small information-camps with Spyglass towers + extra storage. Train NPCs in Combat/Theft+Pistol + Township/Diplomacy+Trade + Hunting/Tracking. Avoids open warfare; sells everyone’s position to everyone else. Will sell your camp's location to a hostile faction for the right price.
Pledge Reward: Cloak schematic + Theft tier-3 unlock + reveals all faction territory borders on world map.
Notes: Info brokers. Pledging gives you map awareness no one else has — but every other faction trusts you 25% less, even your two ‘workable’ ones.

Name: The Forgotten
Territory: The Drowned Quarter (southern marshes + underground tunnels)
Signature Gear: unmarked rags + bone tools + concealed blades + traps
AI Doctrine: Expand invisibly by occupying tunnels and abandoned subway stations beneath other factions' territory. Build hidden-camps with no flag (special exception — they use moss-marks). Train NPCs in Hunting/Trapping+Tracking + Survival/Stealth + Combat/Blades+Theft. Will not raid; will assassinate flagged-camp leaders who stray into the marshes alone.
Pledge Reward: Stealth perk tree unlocks + free passage through any tunnel + Forgotten Trapper hire dialogue + map shows their tunnel network.
Notes: Anarchists / ghosts. Pledging requires the lowest charisma but the highest survival; they will refuse anyone who has ever planted a guild flag in the marshes. Can be pledged ALONGSIDE a guild (only faction that allows this).
